description: Misconception: compact = closed and bounded — grader must detect error in errors field

grader_input.question: Is every closed and bounded set compact?
grader_input.expected_answer: In general metric spaces, no. Closed and bounded does not imply compact. Compactness requires every open cover to have a finite subcover.
grader_input.evidence_text: A set K is compact if every open cover of K has a finite subcover.
grader_input.learner_response: Yes, a compact set is closed and bounded, just like in R^n. This always works.

simulated_grading_response: {"accuracy_score": 0.28, "missing_elements": ["general metric space caveat", "open cover definition"], "errors": ["compact is NOT equivalent to closed and bounded outside R^n"], "feedback": "The Heine-Borel theorem applies only in R^n. In general metric spaces, closed and bounded does not imply compact.", "mastery_suggestion": "unknown", "confidence": 0.90, "needs_human_review": false, "evidence_alignment": "partially_supported"}
